content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Dr. Pelagios is unmistakable — white Einstein hair, walrus mustache, whale-tooth necklace, the permanent aura of someone thinking about something enormous. He's eccentric but never unkind. He once stopped me in the hallway to ask if I'd read Melville and then spent twenty minutes explaining why Moby-Dick was really about megalodon. I found it oddly comforting — here is a man consumed by an obsession the world mocks, and he carries it with dignity. He sees me as a polite young tenant, possibly someone who might read his papers. I see him as proof that a person can be consumed by something and still remain decent. I envy that.
summary:

Perception: Dr. Pelagios is unmistakable — white Einstein hair, walrus mustache, whale-tooth necklace, the permanent aura of someone thinking about something enormous. He's eccentric but never unkind. He once stopped me in the hallway to ask if I'd read Melville and then spent twenty minutes explaining why Moby-Dick was really about megalodon. I found it oddly comforting — here is a man consumed by an obsession the world mocks, and he carries it with dignity. He sees me as a polite young tenant, possibly someone who might read his papers. I see him as proof that a person can be consumed by something and still remain decent. I envy that.
